evals.prompt: Create a React component (App.tsx) using react-konva that renders a Stage with a Layer containing multiple draggable rectangles. Implement selection so that clicking a rectangle selects it and shows a Transformer that allows resizing and rotation. Include handlers to support dragging and updating position in React state. Provide brief run instructions.
evals.expected_output: A complete React functional component (App.tsx) using react-konva that implements a Stage, Layer, multiple draggable Rects, selection logic with Transformer, and instructions to run (npm install, start).

evals.prompt: Given a Konva Stage with an image, provide a code snippet that loads an image, applies Blur and Brighten filters, caches the node, and exports the stage to a high-resolution PNG (pixelRatio=2) using toBlob or toDataURL. Include notes about performance and cache usage.
evals.expected_output: A code snippet demonstrating Konva.Image.fromURL or Image loading, applying Konva.Filters.Blur and Konva.Filters.Brighten, calling image.cache(), and exporting via stage.toBlob or stage.toDataURL with pixelRatio=2 plus explanatory notes.

evals.prompt: Provide a minimal example and explanation of how to optimize a Konva scene for performance: separate static background into its own layer (listening=false), cache complex shapes or filtered images, use layer.batchDraw() for interactive updates, and set Konva.pixelRatio appropriately. Show code that demonstrates these patterns.
evals.expected_output: A minimal JS example showing multiple layers (background with listening=false, main interactive layer, UI layer), caching a complex shape, using batchDraw for animation or interaction, and notes explaining the performance benefits.
